0.original: There is a reason why the you are unimportant attitude prevails.
0.b1: There is a reason why people often think others are not important.
0.cn: 为什么“你并不重要”这种态度会盛行，是有原因的。
0.ai_notes: 'prevails': exists or is very common in a particular place or time.
0.remarks: 'prevails': 盛行，指在特定时间或地点普遍存在。

1.original: Most folks look at another person and think, he can't do anything for me, therefore he's not important.
1.b1: Most people look at others and think, 'He cannot help me, so he is not important.'
1.cn: 大多数人看别人时会想：他对我没用，所以他不重要。
1.ai_notes: 'folks': a friendly or informal way to say 'people'.
1.remarks: 'folks': 人们，一种友好且非正式的称呼。

2.original: But right there is where people make a basic blunder.
2.b1: But this is where people make a big mistake.
2.cn: 但就在这一点上，人们犯了一个根本性的错误。
2.remarks: 'blunder': 大错，指因粗心或愚蠢导致的严重失误。

3.original: The other person, regardless of his status or his income, is important to you,
3.b1: The other person is important to you, no matter what his job or income is,
3.cn: 无论对方的地位或收入如何，他都对你很重要，
3.ai_notes: 'regardless of': without being affected by; no matter what.
3.remarks: 'regardless of': 不管，不顾，表示不受某事影响。

4.original: and for two giant dollars and cents reasons.
4.b1: and there are two big reasons for this.
4.cn: 而且有两个非常重要的原因。
4.ai_notes: 'dollars and cents': an idiom meaning practical, financial, or real-world value.
4.remarks: 'dollars and cents': 实际价值，指金钱层面或现实利益。

5.original: First, people do more for you when you make them feel important.
5.b1: First, people do more for you when you make them feel important.
5.cn: 首先，当你让别人感到自己很重要时，他们会为你做更多的事。
5.ai_notes: 'do more for you': to provide extra help or effort for your benefit.
5.remarks: 'do more for you': 为你多做一点，指提供额外的帮助或努力。

6.original: Years ago, in Detroit, I rode a certain bus to work each morning.
6.b1: Years ago, I took the same bus to work every morning in Detroit.
6.cn: 多年前，在底特律，我每天早上都坐同一辆公交车去上班。
6.ai_notes: 'a certain': used to refer to a specific person or thing without naming them directly.
6.remarks: 'a certain': 某位，指代特定但不直接点名的人或事物。

7.original: The driver was an old grump.
7.b1: The driver was a grumpy old man.
7.cn: 司机是个脾气暴躁的老头。
7.ai_notes: 'grump': a person who is often unhappy, annoyed, or in a bad mood.
7.remarks: 'grump': 脾气暴躁的人，指经常闷闷不乐或爱抱怨的人。

8.original: Dozens, maybe hundreds of times, I saw this driver pull away from the curb when a wildly waving,
8.b1: Many times, I saw the driver leave the bus stop even when a passenger was running,
8.cn: 我看到过很多次，即使有乘客在后面挥手、
8.ai_notes: 'pull away from the curb': to drive a vehicle away from the side of the street where it was parked.
8.remarks: 'pull away from the curb': 驶离路边，指车辆从停车处开走。

9.original: shouting, and running passenger was just a second or two from the door.
9.b1: shouting, and trying to catch the bus.
9.cn: 大喊并奔跑着追车，司机也会直接把车开走。
9.ai_notes: 'a second or two': a very short amount of time.
9.remarks: 'a second or two': 一两秒钟，形容极短的时间。

10.original: Over a period of several months, I saw this driver show special courtesy to only one passenger,
10.b1: Over several months, I saw the driver show special kindness to only one passenger,
10.cn: 几个月里，我发现这位司机只对一位乘客表现出特别的礼貌，
10.ai_notes: 'courtesy': polite behavior or kindness toward others.
10.remarks: 'courtesy': 礼貌，指对他人的客气或善意行为。

11.original: and this passenger was shown special courtesy many times.
11.b1: and this passenger received this kindness many times.
11.cn: 而且这位乘客多次享受到这种优待。
11.ai_notes: 'shown special courtesy': received extra polite or kind treatment.
11.remarks: 'shown special courtesy': 受到特别礼遇，指被给予了额外的尊重或关照。

12.original: The driver would wait for this passenger.
12.b1: The driver would wait for this passenger.
12.cn: 司机总是会等这位乘客。
12.ai_notes: 'wait for': to stay in one place until someone arrives.
12.remarks: 'wait for': 等候，指停留在某处直到某人到来。

13.ai_notes: 'And why?': a rhetorical question used to introduce an explanation.
13.remarks: 'And why?': 为什么呢？一种反问句，用于引出后续的解释。

14.original: Because this passenger went out of his way to make the driver feel important.
14.b1: Because this passenger always tried to make the driver feel important.
14.cn: 因为这位乘客总是特意让司机感到自己很重要。
14.ai_notes: 'went out of his way': to make a special effort to do something, even if it is inconvenient.
14.remarks: 'went out of his way': 特地去做，指为了某事不惜付出额外努力。

15.original: Every morning he greeted the driver with a personalized, sincere, good morning, sir.
15.b1: Every morning, he said 'Good morning, sir' in a kind and personal way.
15.cn: 每天早上，他都会真诚且礼貌地向司机问好。
15.ai_notes: 'personalized': made specifically for one person; not generic.
15.remarks: 'personalized': 个性化的，指专门为某人定制的，而非通用的。

16.original: Sometimes this passenger would sit near the driver and make little comments like, you sure have a lot of responsibility.
16.b1: Sometimes he sat near the driver and said things like, 'You have a very important job.'
16.cn: 有时他会坐在司机旁边说：“你的责任真的很重。”
16.ai_notes: 'make little comments': to say short, casual things to start a conversation.
16.remarks: 'make little comments': 随口聊几句，指通过简短的闲谈来开启对话。

17.original: It must take nerves of steel to drive through traffic like this every day.
17.b1: It must be hard to drive in this traffic every day.
17.cn: “每天在这样的交通状况下开车，一定需要极强的心理素质。”
17.ai_notes: 'nerves of steel': a phrase meaning the ability to stay calm in dangerous or difficult situations.
17.remarks: 'nerves of steel': 钢铁般的神经，形容在困难或危险中保持冷静。

18.original: You sure keep this thing on schedule.
18.b1: You are very good at keeping the bus on time.
18.cn: “你总是能准时发车，真了不起。”
18.ai_notes: 'on schedule': happening at the planned or expected time.
18.remarks: 'on schedule': 按计划，指事情在预定的时间发生。

19.original: That passenger made the driver feel as important as if he were piloting a 180-passenger jet airliner.
19.b1: That passenger made the driver feel as important as a pilot of a large plane.
19.cn: 那位乘客让司机感到自己就像在驾驶一架大型客机一样重要。
19.ai_notes: 'piloting': the act of flying an aircraft.
19.remarks: 'piloting': 驾驶（飞机），指操作飞行器。

20.original: And the driver, in return, showed special courtesy to the passenger.
20.b1: In return, the driver was very kind to him.
20.cn: 作为回报，司机也对这位乘客格外客气。
20.ai_notes: 'in return': as a response or a way of giving back for something received.
20.remarks: 'in return': 作为回报，指对所获事物做出的回应。

21.original: It pays to make little people feel like big people.
21.b1: It is a good idea to make ordinary people feel special.
21.cn: 让普通人感到自己很重要，这非常有价值。
21.ai_notes: 'It pays to': it is beneficial or useful to do something.
21.remarks: 'It pays to': 做某事是值得的，指某行为能带来好处。

22.original: Today, in thousands of offices all over America, secretaries are helping salesmen make sales or lose sales,
22.b1: Today, in many offices, secretaries help salespeople succeed or fail,
22.cn: 今天，在成千上万的办公室里，秘书们决定着销售人员能否成功，
22.ai_notes: 'salesmen': people whose job is to sell products or services.
22.remarks: 'salesmen': 推销员，指以销售产品或服务为职业的人。

23.original: depending on how the salesman has treated them.
23.b1: depending on how the salesperson treats them.
23.cn: 这取决于销售人员如何对待她们。
23.ai_notes: 'depending on': determined by or influenced by.
23.remarks: 'depending on': 取决于，指受某因素的影响或决定。

24.original: Make someone feel important and he cares about you.
24.b1: Make someone feel important, and they will care about you.
24.cn: 让别人感到自己很重要，他们就会关心你。
24.ai_notes: 'cares about': to feel that someone is important and show interest in their well-being.
24.remarks: 'cares about': 关心，指重视某人并关注其福祉。

25.original: And when he cares about you, he does more for you.
25.b1: When they care about you, they will do more for you.
25.cn: 当他们关心你时，他们会为你做更多的事。
25.ai_notes: 'does more for you': provides extra assistance or support.
25.remarks: 'does more for you': 为你做更多，指提供额外的支持。

26.original: Customers will buy more from you.
26.b1: Customers will buy more from you.
26.cn: 客户会从你这里买更多的东西。
26.ai_notes: 'customers': people who buy goods or services from a business.
26.remarks: 'customers': 顾客，指购买商品或服务的人。

27.original: Employees will work harder for you.
27.b1: Employees will work harder for you.
27.cn: 员工会为你更努力地工作。
27.ai_notes: 'employees': people who work for a company or another person.
27.remarks: 'employees': 员工，指受雇于公司或个人的人。

28.original: Associates will go out of their way to cooperate with you.
28.b1: Colleagues will be happy to help you.
28.cn: 同事们会乐意与你合作。
28.ai_notes: 'associates': people you work with; colleagues. 'cooperate': to work together toward a common goal.
28.remarks: 'associates': 同事，指工作伙伴。'cooperate': 合作，指为共同目标一起努力。

29.original: Your boss will do more to help you, if you will only make these people feel important.
29.b1: Your boss will help you more if you make these people feel important.
29.cn: 如果你能让这些人感到自己很重要，你的老板也会更愿意帮助你。
29.ai_notes: 'if you will only': a way of saying that if you simply choose to do this, you will see results.
29.remarks: 'if you will only': 如果你愿意，一种鼓励语气，暗示只要尝试就会有结果。

30.original: It pays to make big people feel even bigger.
30.b1: It is worth it to make important people feel even more important.
30.cn: 让重要人物感到自己更重要，这同样值得。
30.ai_notes: 'big people': people who are successful, powerful, or important.
30.remarks: 'big people': 大人物，指成功、有权势或重要的人。

31.original: The big thinker always adds value to people by visualizing them at their best.
31.b1: A big thinker always adds value to people by seeing the best in them.
31.cn: 善于思考的人总是通过发掘他人的优点来提升他们的价值。
31.ai_notes: 'adds value': to increase the worth or importance of something. 'visualizing': imagining someone in a specific way.
31.remarks: 'adds value': 增加价值，指提升某物的意义。'visualizing': 想象，指在脑海中构思某人的形象。

32.original: Because he thinks big about people, he gets their best out of them.
32.b1: Because he thinks highly of people, he gets the best results from them.
32.cn: 因为他看重他人，所以他能从别人身上得到最好的表现。
32.ai_notes: 'thinks big about': to have a positive and high opinion of someone's potential. 'gets their best out of them': encourages them to perform at their highest level.
32.remarks: 'thinks big about': 对某人寄予厚望。'gets their best out of them': 激发潜力，指促使对方发挥出最高水平。

33.original: Here's the second giant reason for making others feel important.
33.b1: Here is the second big reason to make others feel important.
33.cn: 这就是让别人感到自己很重要的第二个重要原因。
33.ai_notes: 'giant reason': a very significant or important reason.
33.remarks: 'giant reason': 重大原因，指非常关键的理由。
